title: State Pension Credit Act 2002, Section 1
body: A social security benefit to be known as state pension credit shall be payable in accordance with the following provisions of this Act. A claimant is entitled to state pension credit if the claimant is in Great Britain, has attained the qualifying age, and satisfies the condition in section 2(1) for guarantee credit or the conditions in section 3(1) and (2) for savings credit. A claimant who is entitled to state pension credit is entitled to a guarantee credit, calculated in accordance with section 2, or to a savings credit, calculated in accordance with section 3, or to both, if the claimant satisfies both the guarantee-credit condition and the savings-credit conditions.
